title: Present Simple — Habits & Facts

questions.question: She ___ at a hospital.
questions.explanation: With he/she/it we add -s to the verb.

questions.question: They ___ football every Sunday.
questions.explanation: With they/we/you/I we use the base form.

questions.question: ___ she a doctor? (Is / Are / Am)
questions.explanation: With he/she/it we use "Is" for questions.

questions.question: I ___ breakfast every morning.
questions.explanation: With "I" we use the base form of the verb.

questions.question: He ___ to school by bus. (go / goes / going)
questions.explanation: With he/she/it, verbs ending in -o add -es: go → goes.

questions.question: The sun ___ in the east.
questions.explanation: Facts use present simple. With "the sun" (it), we add -s.

questions.question: ___ your brother work here?
questions.explanation: For he/she/it questions we use "Does" + base form.

questions.question: She ___ not like coffee. (do / does / is)
questions.explanation: Negatives with he/she/it use "does not" + base form.

questions.question: We ___ English at school.
questions.explanation: With "we" we use the base form: study.

questions.question: My cat ___ fish every day.
questions.explanation: With he/she/it (my cat = it) we add -s or -es.

questions.question: I ___ to music every night. (listen / listens / listening)
questions.explanation: With "I" we always use the base form.

questions.question: She ___ three languages.
questions.explanation: Ability/fact with she → add -s: speaks.

questions.question: Do they ___ in London?
questions.explanation: After "do/does" we always use the base form.

questions.question: He ___ his teeth twice a day. (brush / brushes / brushing)
questions.explanation: Verbs ending in -sh add -es with he/she/it: brush → brushes.

questions.question: Water ___ at 100 degrees.
questions.explanation: Scientific facts use present simple. Water (it) → boils.

questions.question: I ___ not understand this word.
questions.explanation: With "I" the negative is "do not" (don't).

questions.question: She ___ yoga every morning. (do / does / doing)
questions.explanation: With she/he/it, "do" becomes "does" as a main verb too.

questions.question: My parents ___ in a small town.
questions.explanation: With "my parents" (they) we use the base form: live.

questions.question: ___ he play the guitar? (Do / Does / Is)
questions.explanation: Questions with he/she/it use "Does" at the start.

questions.question: The bank ___ at 9am every day.
questions.explanation: Routine fact with "the bank" (it) → opens.

title: Past Simple — Completed Actions

questions.question: Yesterday I ___ to the market.
questions.explanation: "Go" is irregular. Past simple = went.

questions.question: She ___ a book last night. (read / reads / reading)
questions.explanation: "Read" is irregular — past simple is also spelled "read" but pronounced "red".

questions.question: They ___ a movie on Saturday.
questions.explanation: Regular verbs add -ed in past simple: watch → watched.

questions.question: ___ he call you yesterday?
questions.explanation: Past simple questions use "Did" + base form.

questions.question: I ___ very tired after work. (was / were / am)
questions.explanation: Past of "am/is" is "was". Past of "are" is "were".

questions.question: We ___ dinner at 7pm last night.
questions.explanation: "Have" is irregular. Past simple = had.

questions.question: She ___ not come to class yesterday.
questions.explanation: Past negative = did not (didn't) + base form.

questions.question: He ___ his keys this morning. (lose / lost / loses)
questions.explanation: "Lose" is irregular. Past simple = lost.

questions.question: I ___ a letter to my friend last week.
questions.explanation: "Write" is irregular. Past simple = wrote.

questions.question: They ___ football yesterday afternoon.
questions.explanation: Regular verb: play → played.

questions.question: She ___ happy when she heard the news. (is / was / were)
questions.explanation: Past state with she → was.

questions.question: We ___ to Paris last summer.
questions.explanation: Regular verb: travel → travelled.

questions.question: ___ you enjoy the party?
questions.explanation: Past simple question with "you" → Did you...?

questions.question: He ___ the window by accident. (break / broke / broken)
questions.explanation: "Break" is irregular. Past simple = broke.

questions.question: I ___ up late this morning.
questions.explanation: "Wake" is irregular. Past simple = woke.

questions.question: She ___ her homework before dinner.
questions.explanation: Regular verb: finish → finished.

questions.question: They ___ not go to school yesterday. (do / did / does)
questions.explanation: Past negative: did not + base form.

questions.question: He ___ a new car last month.
questions.explanation: "Buy" is irregular. Past simple = bought.

questions.question: I ___ my friend at the cafe. (meet / met / meets)
questions.explanation: "Meet" is irregular. Past simple = met.

questions.question: The children ___ very loud yesterday.
questions.explanation: Past of "are" (with plural noun) = were.

title: Articles — a, an, the

questions.question: I saw ___ elephant at the zoo.
questions.explanation: Use "an" before vowel sounds: an elephant.

questions.question: She is ___ doctor.
questions.explanation: Use "a" before consonant sounds for jobs: a doctor.

questions.question: Please close ___ door. (a / an / the)
questions.explanation: Use "the" when both people know which thing: the door (this specific door).

questions.question: I have ___ umbrella.
questions.explanation: "Umbrella" starts with a vowel sound → an umbrella.

questions.question: ___ sun rises in the east. (A / An / The)
questions.explanation: Use "the" for unique things — there is only one sun.

questions.question: He wants to be ___ engineer.
questions.explanation: "Engineer" starts with a vowel sound → an engineer.

questions.question: I read ___ interesting book last week.
questions.explanation: "Interesting" starts with a vowel → an interesting book.

questions.question: Can you pass me ___ salt? (a / an / the)
questions.explanation: Use "the" when the listener knows what you mean (the salt on the table).

questions.question: She has ___ cat and ___ dog.
questions.explanation: Both "cat" and "dog" start with consonant sounds → a cat, a dog.

questions.question: ___ Nile is the longest river in Africa.
questions.explanation: Use "the" with river names: the Nile, the Amazon.

questions.question: I need ___ pen. (a / an / the)
questions.explanation: "Pen" starts with a consonant sound → a pen.

questions.question: This is ___ best movie I have ever seen.
questions.explanation: Use "the" with superlatives: the best, the most, the longest.

questions.question: She is ___ honest person. (a / an / the)
questions.explanation: "Honest" starts with a silent H — the sound is a vowel → an honest person.

questions.question: I live on ___ fifth floor.
questions.explanation: Use "the" with ordinal numbers: the fifth floor.

questions.question: We visited ___ Eiffel Tower.
questions.explanation: Use "the" with famous landmarks: the Eiffel Tower.

questions.question: He plays ___ guitar. (a / an / the)
questions.explanation: Use "the" with musical instruments: the guitar, the piano.

questions.question: I had ___ egg for breakfast.
questions.explanation: "Egg" starts with a vowel sound → an egg.

questions.question: She is ___ university student.
questions.explanation: "University" starts with a "y" sound (consonant) → a university.

questions.question: ___ Pacific Ocean is very large. (A / An / The)
questions.explanation: Use "the" with oceans and seas: the Pacific Ocean.

questions.question: Can I ask you ___ question?
questions.explanation: "Question" starts with a consonant sound → a question.

title: Basic Prepositions — on, in, at

questions.question: The book is ___ the table.
questions.explanation: Use "on" for surfaces: on the table, on the floor.

questions.question: She is ___ school right now.
questions.explanation: Use "at" for locations: at school, at work, at home.

questions.question: The keys are ___ my bag. (on / in / at)
questions.explanation: Use "in" for enclosed spaces: in the bag, in the box.

questions.question: I was born ___ 1999.
questions.explanation: Use "in" with years and months: in 1999, in March.

questions.question: The meeting is ___ 3 o'clock. (on / in / at)
questions.explanation: Use "at" with specific times: at 3 o'clock, at noon.

questions.question: My birthday is ___ Monday.
questions.explanation: Use "on" with days of the week: on Monday, on Friday.

questions.question: She lives ___ the second floor. (on / in / at)
questions.explanation: Use "on" with floors: on the second floor, on the top floor.

questions.question: We met ___ the airport.
questions.explanation: Use "at" with specific places/events: at the airport, at the station.

questions.question: He arrived ___ the morning.
questions.explanation: Use "in" with parts of the day: in the morning, in the afternoon.

questions.question: There is a fly ___ the ceiling. (on / in / at)
questions.explanation: Use "on" for surfaces including ceilings and walls.

questions.question: I will see you ___ the weekend.
questions.explanation: Use "at" with the weekend (British English).

questions.question: She is waiting ___ the bus stop.
questions.explanation: Use "at" for specific points/locations: at the bus stop.

questions.question: The children are playing ___ the garden. (on / in / at)
questions.explanation: Use "in" for enclosed or defined spaces: in the garden.

questions.question: He was born ___ January.
questions.explanation: Use "in" with months: in January, in December.

questions.question: I always watch TV ___ night. (on / in / at)
questions.explanation: Use "at" with night: at night (but "in the morning/evening").

questions.question: Put the milk ___ the fridge.
questions.explanation: Use "in" for containers and enclosed spaces: in the fridge.

questions.question: She is ___ work right now. (on / in / at)
questions.explanation: Use "at" for places where you do activities: at work, at school.

questions.question: The picture is ___ the wall.
questions.explanation: Use "on" for things attached to surfaces: on the wall.

questions.question: We arrived ___ Christmas.
questions.explanation: Use "at" with holidays: at Christmas, at Easter.

questions.question: I read ___ the evening. (on / in / at)
questions.explanation: Use "in" with parts of the day except night: in the evening.

title: Common Opposites & Adjectives

questions.question: "Hot" is the opposite of:
questions.explanation: "Cold" is the direct opposite of "hot".

questions.question: "Fast" is the opposite of:
questions.explanation: "Slow" is the direct opposite of "fast".

questions.question: "Happy" is the opposite of:
questions.explanation: "Sad" is the direct opposite of "happy".

questions.question: "Big" is the opposite of:
questions.explanation: "Small" is the direct opposite of "big".

questions.question: "Old" is the opposite of (for people):
questions.explanation: "Young" is the opposite of "old" for people.

questions.question: "Expensive" means:
questions.explanation: "Expensive" means something costs a lot of money.

questions.question: "Difficult" is the opposite of:
questions.explanation: "Easy" is the opposite of "difficult".

questions.question: "Dark" is the opposite of ___. (bright / small / quiet)
questions.explanation: "Bright" (or "light") is the opposite of "dark".

questions.question: "Noisy" means:
questions.explanation: "Noisy" means making a lot of sound — the opposite of quiet.

questions.question: "Hungry" means:
questions.explanation: "Hungry" means you want to eat food.

questions.question: "Clean" is the opposite of ___. (wet / dirty / old)
questions.explanation: "Dirty" is the opposite of "clean".

questions.question: "Thirsty" means:
questions.explanation: "Thirsty" means you want to drink something.

questions.question: "Strong" is the opposite of:
questions.explanation: "Weak" is the opposite of "strong".

questions.question: "Early" is the opposite of ___. (slow / late / fast)
questions.explanation: "Late" is the opposite of "early".

questions.question: "Brave" means:
questions.explanation: "Brave" means not afraid — you do things even when they are scary.

questions.question: "Full" is the opposite of:
questions.explanation: "Empty" is the opposite of "full".

questions.question: "Interesting" is the opposite of ___. (boring / difficult / quiet)
questions.explanation: "Boring" is the opposite of "interesting".

questions.question: "Polite" means:
questions.explanation: "Polite" means having good manners — saying please, thank you, excuse me.

questions.question: "Generous" means:
questions.explanation: "Generous" means you like to give things to other people.

questions.question: "Tall" is the opposite of ___. (big / short / thin)
questions.explanation: "Short" is the opposite of "tall" for height.

title: Can & Can't — Ability

questions.question: She ___ speak three languages.
questions.explanation: "Can" never changes — no -s, no -ing. She can (not "she cans").

questions.question: I ___ swim. I never learned. (can / can't / cans)
questions.explanation: "Can't" (cannot) means you are not able to do something.

questions.question: ___ you ride a bike?
questions.explanation: Ability questions use "Can" at the start.

questions.question: Birds ___ fly.
questions.explanation: Fact about ability: birds can fly.

questions.question: He ___ drive yet. He is only 15. (can / can't / could)
questions.explanation: Use "can't" for something not possible or not allowed.

questions.question: I ___ hear you. Please speak louder.
questions.explanation: "Can't hear" means the sound is not reaching you.

questions.question: She ___ play the piano very well.
questions.explanation: Positive ability: she can play the piano.

questions.question: ___ cats swim? (Do / Can / Are)
questions.explanation: Ability question about cats → Can cats swim? (Yes, they can.)

questions.question: We ___ see the mountains from here.
questions.explanation: Positive ability/perception: we can see the mountains.

questions.question: Fish ___ breathe in water.
questions.explanation: Fact: fish can breathe in water — they have gills.

questions.question: I ___ find my keys anywhere! (can / can't / could)
questions.explanation: "Can't find" means you are unable to find something.

questions.question: My grandfather ___ use a smartphone.
questions.explanation: "Can't" for inability — he doesn't know how.

questions.question: ___ I help you with that?
questions.explanation: "Can I...?" is used to offer help politely.

questions.question: She ___ run very fast. She wins every race. (can / can't / does)
questions.explanation: Positive ability: she can run fast.

questions.question: I ___ believe it! That's amazing!
questions.explanation: "Can't believe" is a common expression of surprise.

questions.question: Babies ___ walk when they are born. (can / can't / could)
questions.explanation: Fact: babies cannot walk when they are born.

questions.question: He ___ cook really well. His food is delicious.
questions.explanation: Positive ability: he can cook well.

questions.question: Sorry, I ___ come to the party tonight.
questions.explanation: "Can't come" means you are not able to attend.

questions.question: ___ you help me please? (Do / Can / Are)
questions.explanation: "Can you...?" is a polite way to ask for help.

questions.question: Elephants ___ jump.
questions.explanation: Fact: elephants cannot jump — they are too heavy.

title: Present Continuous — Right Now

questions.question: She ___ dinner right now.
questions.explanation: Present continuous = am/is/are + verb-ing. For actions happening right now.

questions.question: They ___ football at the moment. (play / are playing / plays)
questions.explanation: With "they" use "are" + verb-ing.

questions.question: ___ you listening to me?
questions.explanation: Present continuous question with "you" → Are you...?

questions.question: I ___ a book this week.
questions.explanation: Present continuous for temporary situations: am reading this week.

questions.question: He ___ not working today. (is / are / am)
questions.explanation: Negative present continuous with he: is not + verb-ing.

questions.question: Look! It ___ outside.
questions.explanation: "Look!" signals right now → is raining.

questions.question: We ___ for the bus right now.
questions.explanation: With "we" use "are" + verb-ing.

questions.question: The baby ___. (is sleeping / sleeps / sleep)
questions.explanation: Present continuous with "the baby" (it/she/he): is + verb-ing.

questions.question: Why ___ you wearing a coat? It's hot!
questions.explanation: Present continuous question with "you" → are you...?

questions.question: She ___ her hair right now.
questions.explanation: Action right now with she → is washing.

questions.question: I ___ to music. Please don't disturb me. (listen / am listening / listens)
questions.explanation: With "I" use "am" + verb-ing for present continuous.

questions.question: The students ___ a test at the moment.
questions.explanation: With plural subject use "are" + verb-ing.

questions.question: He ___ not feeling well today.
questions.explanation: Negative present continuous with he: is not + verb-ing.

questions.question: What ___ you doing? (are / is / do)
questions.explanation: Present continuous question with "you" → What are you doing?

questions.question: My parents ___ dinner right now.
questions.explanation: With "my parents" (they) use "are" + verb-ing.

questions.question: I ___ living with my sister this month.
questions.explanation: With "I" use "am" for present continuous.

questions.question: The dog ___ in the garden. (is running / runs / run)
questions.explanation: Action happening right now with "the dog" (it): is + running.

questions.question: ___ she coming to the meeting?
questions.explanation: Present continuous question with she → Is she...?

questions.question: They ___ a new house right now. (build / are building / builds)
questions.explanation: Temporary action in progress with "they": are + verb-ing.

questions.question: I can't talk now. I ___ a shower.
questions.explanation: "Have" can be used as an action verb: am having a shower.

title: Past Continuous — Action in Progress

questions.question: I ___ TV when she called.
questions.explanation: Past continuous = was/were + verb-ing. Action in progress when interrupted.

questions.question: They ___ when I arrived. (sleep / were sleeping / slept)
questions.explanation: With "they" use "were" + verb-ing for past continuous.

questions.question: She ___ a book all afternoon.
questions.explanation: Action in progress over a period in the past: was reading.

questions.question: What ___ you doing at 8pm last night?
questions.explanation: Past continuous question with "you" → were you + verb-ing.

questions.question: He ___ when the earthquake happened. (drive / was driving / drove)
questions.explanation: Interrupted action in past: was driving (when something happened).

questions.question: We ___ dinner when the lights went out.
questions.explanation: Action interrupted by another event: were having dinner.

questions.question: ___ she working late yesterday?
questions.explanation: Past continuous question with "she" → Was she + verb-ing?

questions.question: The children ___ in the park all morning. (play / played / were playing)
questions.explanation: Action in progress over a period in past: were playing.

questions.question: I ___ not feeling well yesterday.
questions.explanation: Past continuous negative with I: was not + verb-ing.

questions.question: While I ___ the dishes, someone knocked.
questions.explanation: "While" + past continuous shows a background action.

questions.question: They ___ loudly in the library. (talk / were talking / talked)
questions.explanation: Past continuous: were + verb-ing for past in-progress action.

questions.question: She ___ her homework when I called.
questions.explanation: Interrupted past action: was doing homework when called.

questions.question: At midnight, he ___ still working.
questions.explanation: "At midnight" tells us a specific past time: was + verb-ing.

questions.question: We ___ about you when you walked in! (talk / were talking / talked)
questions.explanation: Action in progress at the moment of interruption: were talking.

questions.question: ___ they waiting for a long time?
questions.explanation: Past continuous question with "they" → Were they + verb-ing?

questions.question: The sun ___ when we left the house.
questions.explanation: Background condition at a past time: was shining.

questions.question: I ___ at 6am this morning. (sleep / was sleeping / slept)
questions.explanation: Action in progress at a specific past time: was sleeping.

questions.question: She fell asleep while she ___ TV.
questions.explanation: "While" + past continuous for background action: was watching.

questions.question: He ___ his phone when he crashed. (use / was using / used)
questions.explanation: Interrupted action: was using his phone when the crash happened.

questions.question: What ___ they talking about?
questions.explanation: Past continuous question with "they" → What were they talking about?

title: Comparatives & Superlatives

questions.question: An elephant is ___ than a cat.
questions.explanation: Short adjectives add -er for comparatives: big → bigger.

questions.question: This is ___ movie I have ever seen!
questions.explanation: Superlatives use "the" + adjective + -est (or most): the best.

questions.question: She is ___ than her sister. (tall / taller / tallest)
questions.explanation: Comparing two people: use comparative -er form.

questions.question: Today is ___ than yesterday.
questions.explanation: Short adjective ending CVC: double last letter + er: hot → hotter.

questions.question: This test is ___ than the last one.
questions.explanation: Long adjectives (3+ syllables) use "more" for comparatives.

questions.question: He is the ___ student in the class. (smart / smarter / smartest)
questions.explanation: Superlative with short adjective: smart → smartest.

questions.question: This bag is ___ than that one.
questions.explanation: Adjectives ending in -y change to -ier: heavy → heavier.

questions.question: Mount Everest is ___ mountain in the world.
questions.explanation: Superlative: the highest — use "the" with superlatives.

questions.question: Gold is ___ than silver. (expensive / more expensive / most expensive)
questions.explanation: Long adjective comparative: more expensive.

questions.question: My English is getting ___ every day.
questions.explanation: "Good" is irregular: good → better → best.

questions.question: This is ___ I have ever eaten!
questions.explanation: Long adjective superlative: the most delicious.

questions.question: January is ___ than June in England. (cold / colder / coldest)
questions.explanation: Comparative short adjective: cold → colder.

questions.question: She runs ___ than her brother.
questions.explanation: Short adjective: fast → faster.

questions.question: This is ___ road in the city.
questions.explanation: Long adjective superlative: the most dangerous.

questions.question: He is the ___ person I know. (funny / funnier / funniest)
questions.explanation: Superlative: -y changes to -iest: funny → funniest.

questions.question: Traffic is ___ in the morning than at noon.
questions.explanation: "Bad" is irregular: bad → worse → worst.

questions.question: This exercise is ___ than the last one. (easy / easier / easiest)
questions.explanation: Comparative -y adjective: easy → easier.

questions.question: He is ___ person in the office.
questions.explanation: Superlative -y adjective: busy → the busiest.

questions.question: I feel ___ today than yesterday.
questions.explanation: Irregular comparative: bad → worse.

questions.question: This is ___ book in the library. (long / longer / the longest)
questions.explanation: Superlative: the longest — use "the" with all superlatives.

title: Modal Verbs — must, should, might

questions.question: You ___ wear a seatbelt. It's the law.
questions.explanation: "Must" expresses strong obligation or necessity.

questions.question: She ___ be tired. She worked all night.
questions.explanation: "Must" for logical deduction — we are almost sure.

questions.question: You look sick. You ___ see a doctor. (must / should / might)
questions.explanation: "Should" gives advice — it is a good idea.

questions.question: It ___ rain later. The sky looks cloudy.
questions.explanation: "Might" expresses possibility — we are not sure.

questions.question: You ___ smoke in this building.
questions.explanation: "Mustn't" = prohibition. It is not allowed.

questions.question: I ___ be late — I'm not sure. (must / might / should)
questions.explanation: "Might" = possibility, uncertainty.

questions.question: Students ___ bring their ID cards.
questions.explanation: "Must" for strong rules and obligations.

questions.question: You ___ eat more vegetables. It's good for you.
questions.explanation: "Should" for advice and recommendations.

questions.question: She ___ be home — her lights are on. (must / might / should)
questions.explanation: "Must" for near-certain deduction from evidence.

questions.question: I ___ come to the party — I'll try.
questions.explanation: "Might" = possible but not certain.

questions.question: You ___ run in the hospital.
questions.explanation: "Mustn't" for strong prohibition — it is forbidden.

questions.question: He ___ know the answer. He studied hard. (must / might / should)
questions.explanation: "Must" for confident deduction.

questions.question: You ___ tell anyone. It's a secret!
questions.explanation: "Mustn't" for prohibition — do not do this.

questions.question: I think you ___ apologize to her.
questions.explanation: "Should" for advice about the right thing to do.

questions.question: It ___ snow tonight. It's very cold. (must / might / should)
questions.explanation: "Might" for uncertain possibility.

questions.question: All passengers ___ fasten their seatbelts.
questions.explanation: "Must" for rules and strong obligations.

questions.question: She ___ be French. She speaks perfectly.
questions.explanation: "Must" for near-certain deduction.

questions.question: You ___ worry so much. It's not good for you. (must / shouldn't / might)
questions.explanation: "Shouldn't" for negative advice — this is not a good idea.

questions.question: He ___ be a doctor. He's only 18.
questions.explanation: "Can't" for impossibility — we are sure it is not true.

questions.question: We ___ be quiet in the library. (must / might / can)
questions.explanation: "Must" for rules — being quiet in the library is a rule.

title: Conjunctions — and, but, because, so

questions.question: I was tired ___ I went to bed early.
questions.explanation: "So" shows a result: I was tired → so I went to bed.

questions.question: She likes tea ___ she doesn't like coffee.
questions.explanation: "But" shows contrast between two opposite ideas.

questions.question: I stayed home ___ it was raining. (so / but / because)
questions.explanation: "Because" gives a reason: I stayed home because of the rain.

questions.question: He studied hard ___ he passed the exam.
questions.explanation: "So" shows a result of studying hard.

questions.question: I like cats ___ dogs.
questions.explanation: "And" adds two similar ideas together.

questions.question: She is rich ___ she is not happy. (and / but / so)
questions.explanation: "But" shows an unexpected contrast.

questions.question: I was hungry ___ I ate a sandwich.
questions.explanation: "So" = result. Being hungry led to eating.

questions.question: He didn't come ___ he was sick.
questions.explanation: "Because" gives the reason for not coming.

questions.question: You can have cake ___ ice cream. (and / but / because)
questions.explanation: "And" joins two options you can both have.

questions.question: I wanted to go out ___ it was raining.
questions.explanation: "But" shows contrast — wanting to go out vs. rain stopping you.

questions.question: She forgot her umbrella ___ she got wet. (because / so / but)
questions.explanation: "So" = result. Forgetting umbrella → got wet.

questions.question: I drink coffee ___ I need energy.
questions.explanation: "Because" gives the reason for drinking coffee.

questions.question: She can speak English ___ French.
questions.explanation: "And" joins two abilities she has.

questions.question: He is young ___ very talented. (but / so / because)
questions.explanation: "But" shows surprise — young but already talented.

questions.question: The movie was boring ___ we left early.
questions.explanation: "So" = result. Boring movie → left early.

questions.question: I love this song ___ it reminds me of home.
questions.explanation: "Because" = reason for loving the song.

questions.question: She worked hard ___ she didn't get promoted. (but / so / because)
questions.explanation: "But" shows an unexpected contrast — hard work but no promotion.

questions.question: I was late ___ I missed the bus.
questions.explanation: "Because" = reason for being late.

questions.question: It was cold ___ we stayed inside. (but / because / so)
questions.explanation: "So" = result. Cold weather → stayed inside.

questions.question: He likes swimming ___ he hates running.
questions.explanation: "But" contrasts two opposite preferences.

title: Prepositions of Place — in, on, at, under, next to, between

questions.question: The cat is ___ the sofa.
questions.explanation: "Under" = below the sofa, beneath it.

questions.question: The bank is ___ the post office and the pharmacy.
questions.explanation: "Between" = in the middle of two things.

questions.question: The supermarket is ___ the school. (next to / between / above)
questions.explanation: "Next to" = beside, at the side of.

questions.question: There is a lamp ___ the desk.
questions.explanation: "Above" = higher than, over the desk.

questions.question: The dog is hiding ___ the bed.
questions.explanation: "Under" = beneath, below the bed.

questions.question: She is standing ___ the door. (in front of / under / between)
questions.explanation: "In front of" = facing something, before it.

questions.question: The keys are ___ the bowl.
questions.explanation: "In" for inside a container: in the bowl.

questions.question: My house is ___ the park.
questions.explanation: "Next to" = beside, at the side of.

questions.question: He sat ___ me and my sister. (next to / between / behind)
questions.explanation: "Between" = in the middle of two people.

questions.question: The painting is ___ the fireplace.
questions.explanation: "Above" = higher up, over the fireplace.

questions.question: There is a garden ___ the house.
questions.explanation: "Behind" = at the back of, at the rear.

questions.question: The bus stop is ___ the corner. (on / in / at)
questions.explanation: "On the corner" is the correct preposition for street corners.

questions.question: She is ___ the phone right now.
questions.explanation: "On the phone" = using the phone (fixed expression).

questions.question: The children are playing ___ the tree.
questions.explanation: "Under the tree" = in the shade below the tree.

questions.question: The café is ___ the cinema. (next to / between / above)
questions.explanation: "Next to" = beside.

questions.question: He lives ___ the third floor.
questions.explanation: "On the third floor" — use "on" with floors.

questions.question: There is a bridge ___ the river.
questions.explanation: "Over" = from one side to the other, above and across.

questions.question: The answer is ___ the book, on page 10. (in / on / at)
questions.explanation: "In the book" — use "in" for books and documents.

questions.question: She sat ___ me in class.
questions.explanation: "Behind" = at the back of someone.

questions.question: The moon is ___ the clouds tonight. (above / under / next to)
questions.explanation: "Above" = higher than, over the clouds.

title: Present Perfect Simple — Experience & Recent Past

questions.question: I ___ to Japan before.
questions.explanation: Present perfect for life experience (no specific time): have been.

questions.question: She ___ her homework yet. (didn't finish / hasn't finished / won't finish)
questions.explanation: "Yet" with present perfect for something not done but expected.

questions.question: Have you ___ sushi?
questions.explanation: Present perfect = have/has + past participle: eaten.

questions.question: He ___ just arrived home.
questions.explanation: "Just" with present perfect for very recent past: has just arrived.

questions.question: I ___ this movie three times. (saw / have seen / see)
questions.explanation: Repeated experience (no specific time) → present perfect.

questions.question: She has lived here ___ 2010.
questions.explanation: "Since" + specific time point with present perfect.

questions.question: They have been married ___ 10 years.
questions.explanation: "For" + duration of time with present perfect.

questions.question: Have you ___ been to Europe? (ever / yet / just)
questions.explanation: "Ever" in present perfect questions means "at any time in your life".

questions.question: I have ___ seen that film. It's amazing.
questions.explanation: "Already" = before now, sooner than expected.

questions.question: She has ___ met anyone famous.
questions.explanation: "Never" with present perfect for zero experience.

questions.question: He ___ broken his leg twice. (had / has / have)
questions.explanation: With he/she/it use "has" + past participle.

questions.question: I haven't eaten anything ___
questions.explanation: "Yet" at the end of a negative sentence: haven't eaten yet.

questions.question: ___ you ever tried Thai food? (Did / Have / Do)
questions.explanation: Present perfect question for life experience: Have you ever...?

questions.question: She has worked here ___ she was 20.
questions.explanation: "Since" + starting point in time.

questions.question: We have ___ finished — just 5 minutes ago.
questions.explanation: "Just" = very recently completed.

questions.question: I have known her ___ many years. (since / for / ago)
questions.explanation: "For" + duration: for many years.

questions.question: Has he ___ his keys again?
questions.explanation: Present perfect: has + past participle: lost.

questions.question: I have ___ been to that restaurant. Let's go somewhere new.
questions.explanation: "Already" = done before, no need to do again.

questions.question: She has ___ to Paris three times. (go / went / been)
questions.explanation: "Have been to" = visited. Use "been" not "gone" when you've returned.

questions.question: They have lived in this house ___ 20 years.
questions.explanation: "For" + period of time: for 20 years.

title: Future — will & going to

questions.question: Look at those clouds! It ___ rain.
questions.explanation: "Going to" for predictions based on evidence we can see now.

questions.question: I'll have the pasta, please. — I ___ order for you.
questions.explanation: "Will" for decisions made at the moment of speaking.

questions.question: She ___ study medicine next year. (will / is going to / goes)
questions.explanation: "Going to" for plans already decided before speaking.

questions.question: I promise I ___ be late again.
questions.explanation: "Won't" (will not) for promises and decisions.

questions.question: We ___ visit Paris next summer. We already booked.
questions.explanation: "Going to" for plans already made.

questions.question: I think it ___ be a good movie. (will / is going to / would)
questions.explanation: "Will" for predictions based on opinion, not evidence.

questions.question: He ___ to be a doctor when he grows up.
questions.explanation: "Going to" for future intention and plans.

questions.question: Don't worry, I ___ help you.
questions.explanation: "Will" for spontaneous offers and promises.

questions.question: She ___ move to London. She found a job there. (will / is going to / has)
questions.explanation: "Going to" — she has already decided and found a job.

questions.question: I think she ___ win the competition.
questions.explanation: "Will" for personal opinions about the future.

questions.question: Watch out! You ___ fall! (will / are going to / would)
questions.explanation: "Going to" for imminent events based on clear evidence.

questions.question: ___ you help me with this?
questions.explanation: "Will" for requests: Will you...?

questions.question: I ___ call you back in five minutes.
questions.explanation: "Will" for a decision made right now.

questions.question: They ___ get married in June. They've set the date. (will / are going to / have)
questions.explanation: "Going to" — they have already planned and set a date.

questions.question: I'm sure you ___ love this book.
questions.explanation: "Will" for confident predictions based on opinion.

questions.question: She looks exhausted. She ___ collapse!
questions.explanation: "Going to" based on visible evidence (she looks exhausted).

questions.question: I ___ never speak to him again! (will / won't / am going to)
questions.explanation: "Won't" for strong refusals or negative decisions.

questions.question: My parents ___ visit us next month. It's planned.
questions.explanation: "Going to" for arrangements already made.

questions.question: Someone's at the door. I ___ get it. (will / am going to / go)
questions.explanation: "Will" for spontaneous decisions made at the moment.

questions.question: I predict robots ___ do most jobs by 2050.
questions.explanation: "Will" for predictions about the distant future.

title: First Conditional — Real Possibilities

questions.question: If it rains, we ___ stay inside.
questions.explanation: First conditional: if + present simple, will + base verb.

questions.question: If you ___ hard, you will pass. (study / studied / will study)
questions.explanation: The "if" clause uses present simple (not will).

questions.question: She will be upset if you ___ her.
questions.explanation: After "if" use present simple: forget.

questions.question: If I see him, I ___ give him your message.
questions.explanation: Result clause uses "will" + base verb.

questions.question: If you don't hurry, you ___ miss the bus. (miss / will miss / would miss)
questions.explanation: First conditional result: will + base verb.

questions.question: ___ you call me if you need help?
questions.explanation: First conditional question: will + subject.

questions.question: If the weather is nice, we ___ have a picnic.
questions.explanation: Realistic future condition → first conditional → will have.

questions.question: She will feel better if she ___ some rest. (get / gets / will get)
questions.explanation: "If" clause: present simple (gets), result: will feel.

questions.question: If you eat too much, you ___ feel sick.
questions.explanation: Predictable result of a real condition: will feel.

questions.question: I will be surprised if she ___ the job.
questions.explanation: After "if" → present simple (gets).

questions.question: If he ___ the exam, he will be so happy. (pass / passes / will pass)
questions.explanation: "If" clause: present simple (passes).

questions.question: What will you do if you ___ the lottery?
questions.explanation: "If" clause always present simple in first conditional.

questions.question: Unless you apologize, she ___ forgive you. (won't / will / wouldn't)
questions.explanation: "Unless" = if not. She will not forgive unless you apologize.

questions.question: If you don't sleep enough, you ___ be tired.
questions.explanation: Predictable real consequence: will be.

questions.question: She ___ come if you invite her.
questions.explanation: Result of a real future condition: will come.

questions.question: If it ___ too hot, we can use the air conditioning. (is / will be / was)
questions.explanation: "If" clause uses present simple for first conditional.

questions.question: I ___ be angry if you are late again.
questions.explanation: Real future consequence: will be angry.

questions.question: If she ___ her keys, she can't enter. (loses / lost / will lose)
questions.explanation: "If" clause: present simple (loses).

questions.question: You will feel healthier if you ___ exercising.
questions.explanation: "If" clause: present simple (start).

questions.question: If you need anything, just ___ me. (call / called / will call)
questions.explanation: Imperative in result clause is also possible in first conditional.

title: Second Conditional — Imaginary Situations

questions.question: If I had a million dollars, I ___ travel the world.
questions.explanation: Second conditional: if + past simple, would + base verb. For imaginary situations.

questions.question: If she ___ harder, she would pass. (study / studied / studies)
questions.explanation: The "if" clause uses past simple in second conditional.

questions.question: I would buy a house if I ___ enough money.
questions.explanation: After "if" use past simple: had.

questions.question: If you ___ me, what would you do?
questions.explanation: Second conditional uses "were" for all persons (I/he/she/it were).

questions.question: She would be happier if she ___ a better job. (has / had / will have)
questions.explanation: If clause: past simple (had) for unreal condition.

questions.question: What ___ you do if you saw a ghost?
questions.explanation: Second conditional question: what would you do if...?

questions.question: If I ___ you, I would apologize.
questions.explanation: "If I were you" is a fixed expression for giving advice.

questions.question: He would exercise more if he ___ more time. (has / had / will have)
questions.explanation: Imaginary condition: if he had more time (but he doesn't).

questions.question: If animals ___ talk, what would they say?
questions.explanation: Second conditional: if + could (past of can) for imaginary ability.

questions.question: I wouldn't do that if I ___ you.
questions.explanation: "If I were you" — "were" is correct for all subjects in second conditional.

questions.question: If it ___ warmer, we could go swimming. (is / was / were)
questions.explanation: "Were" is more formal/correct in second conditional for all subjects.

questions.question: She would call you if she ___ your number.
questions.explanation: If clause: past simple (knew) for unreal condition.

questions.question: If I ___ a bird, I would fly everywhere. (am / was / were)
questions.explanation: Imaginary/impossible condition: if I were a bird.

questions.question: Would you move abroad if you ___ the chance?
questions.explanation: If clause: past simple (got) for hypothetical opportunity.

questions.question: If he ___ more careful, he wouldn't make mistakes.
questions.explanation: Second conditional: were + more careful for imaginary improvement.

questions.question: I would tell you if I ___. (know / knew / will know)
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: if I knew (but I don't know).

questions.question: If you could live anywhere, where ___ you live?
questions.explanation: Second conditional: where would you...?

questions.question: She ___ feel less stressed if she took a holiday. (will / would / should)
questions.explanation: Result clause: would + base verb.

questions.question: If I won the lottery, I ___ give money to charity.
questions.explanation: Imaginary result: would + base verb.

questions.question: What would happen if everyone ___ meat? (stop eating / stopped eating / stops eating)
questions.explanation: If clause: past simple for hypothetical world condition.

title: Passive Voice — is made, was built

questions.question: This car ___ in Germany.
questions.explanation: Passive voice: am/is/are + past participle. The car is the receiver of the action.

questions.question: The Eiffel Tower ___ in 1889. (built / was built / is built)
questions.explanation: Past passive: was/were + past participle.

questions.question: English ___ all over the world.
questions.explanation: Present passive for general facts: is/are + past participle.

questions.question: The letter ___ yesterday.
questions.explanation: Past passive for completed actions: was/were + past participle.

questions.question: The windows ___ every week. (clean / are cleaned / cleaned)
questions.explanation: Present passive plural: are + past participle.

questions.question: Hamlet ___ by Shakespeare.
questions.explanation: Past passive with "by" to show the agent (who did it).

questions.question: The results ___ tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Future passive: will be + past participle.

questions.question: Rice ___ in many Asian countries. (grow / is grown / grew)
questions.explanation: Present passive for general facts: is + past participle.

questions.question: The thief ___ by the police last night.
questions.explanation: Past passive: was + past participle + by the police.

questions.question: The report must ___ by Friday. (finish / be finished / finished)
questions.explanation: Modal passive: must/should/can + be + past participle.

questions.question: These shoes ___ in Italy.
questions.explanation: Present passive plural: are + past participle.

questions.question: The movie ___ directed by Steven Spielberg.
questions.explanation: Past passive with "by": was + past participle + by the director.

questions.question: Thousands of cars ___ every day. (sell / are sold / sold)
questions.explanation: Present passive plural for regular facts: are + past participle.

questions.question: The meeting has ___ cancelled.
questions.explanation: Present perfect passive: has/have + been + past participle.

questions.question: The package ___ delivered tomorrow. (will / will be / is)
questions.explanation: Future passive: will be + past participle.

questions.question: The pyramids ___ by the ancient Egyptians.
questions.explanation: Past passive + by: were built by the Egyptians.

questions.question: A new hospital ___ being built in our town.
questions.explanation: Present continuous passive: is/are + being + past participle.

questions.question: French ___ in many African countries. (speak / is spoken / spoke)
questions.explanation: Present passive for language facts: is + past participle.

questions.question: The suspect ___ questioned by the police.
questions.explanation: Present continuous passive: is being + past participle (happening now).

questions.question: The contract ___ signed last week. (sign / was signed / is signed)
questions.explanation: Past passive for completed action: was + past participle.

title: Common Phrasal Verbs

questions.question: I need to ___ smoking. It's bad for my health.
questions.explanation: "Give up" = stop doing something permanently.

questions.question: She ___ her children while working full time. (looks after / looks up / looks out)
questions.explanation: "Look after" = take care of someone or something.

questions.question: I couldn't ___ what he was saying. It was too noisy.
questions.explanation: "Make out" = understand or hear something with difficulty.

questions.question: The plane ___ two hours late.
questions.explanation: "Take off" = when a plane leaves the ground and flies.

questions.question: I ___ an interesting article about climate change. (came across / came up / came over)
questions.explanation: "Come across" = find something by chance.

questions.question: She ___ her mother — they look exactly the same!
questions.explanation: "Take after" = resemble a family member in looks or personality.

questions.question: Don't ___ — we can solve this problem together.
questions.explanation: "Give up" = stop trying, quit.

questions.question: I need to ___ this word in the dictionary. (look up / look after / look out)
questions.explanation: "Look up" = search for information in a book or online.

questions.question: They ___ their meeting until next week.
questions.explanation: "Put off" = postpone, delay something to a later time.

questions.question: The fire ___ in the middle of the night.
questions.explanation: "Break out" = start suddenly (fire, war, disease).

questions.question: My car ___ on the way to work. (broke down / broke out / broke up)
questions.explanation: "Break down" = stop working (machine or vehicle).

questions.question: Can you ___ the TV? I'm trying to sleep.
questions.explanation: "Turn off" = switch off a machine or device.

questions.question: She ___ a new hobby — painting. (took up / took off / took after)
questions.explanation: "Take up" = start a new hobby or activity.

questions.question: I ___ my old friend at the supermarket.
questions.explanation: "Run into" = meet someone by accident.

questions.question: We ___ milk. Can you buy some?
questions.explanation: "Run out of" = have no more of something.

questions.question: Please ___ your shoes before entering. (take off / take up / take after)
questions.explanation: "Take off" = remove clothing or accessories.

questions.question: The music is too loud. Can you ___ it ___?
questions.explanation: "Turn down" = reduce the volume or level of something.

questions.question: I ___ early so I could catch the train. (got up / got in / got over)
questions.explanation: "Get up" = get out of bed and start the day.

questions.question: She ___ her illness very quickly.
questions.explanation: "Get over" = recover from an illness or difficult situation.

questions.question: He ___ the exam results — he passed! (found out / found up / found in)
questions.explanation: "Find out" = discover or learn information.

title: Present Perfect Continuous — How Long?

questions.question: I ___ here for three hours.
questions.explanation: Present perfect continuous: have/has + been + verb-ing. For ongoing actions.

questions.question: She ___ all day and she's exhausted. (works / has been working / worked)
questions.explanation: Action that started in the past and continues or just stopped: has been working.

questions.question: How long ___ you learning English?
questions.explanation: "How long have you been...?" is the standard question form.

questions.question: They ___ for 20 minutes. Let's start the meeting.
questions.explanation: Duration of ongoing action: have been waiting for 20 minutes.

questions.question: It ___ since this morning. (rains / has been raining / rained)
questions.explanation: Continuous action from a point in the past until now: has been raining since.

questions.question: He looks tired. He ___ all night.
questions.explanation: Recent continuous action explaining current state: has been studying.

questions.question: I ___ to learn guitar for months. (try / have been trying / tried)
questions.explanation: Ongoing effort over a period: have been trying.

questions.question: We ___ this project since January.
questions.explanation: "Since" + point in time with present perfect continuous.

questions.question: She ___ in London for five years now.
questions.explanation: Duration with present perfect continuous: for five years.

questions.question: Why are your eyes red? ___ you crying? (Were / Have / Have been)
questions.explanation: Present perfect continuous question: Have you been crying?

questions.question: I ___ feel well lately.
questions.explanation: "Haven't been feeling" — present perfect continuous negative.

questions.question: He ___ the same job for 10 years. (has / has been doing / did)
questions.explanation: Ongoing situation over a long period: has been doing.

questions.question: What ___ you been doing all day?
questions.explanation: "What have you been doing?" — present perfect continuous question.

questions.question: The children ___ for hours. They must be tired.
questions.explanation: Recent continuous action explaining current state.

questions.question: I ___ a headache all morning. (have / have had / have been having)
questions.explanation: For states (headache, pain) use present perfect simple, not continuous.

questions.question: She ___ Spanish classes since September.
questions.explanation: "Since" + start point with present perfect continuous.

questions.question: They ___ about the problem for hours without a solution. (talk / talked / have been talking)
questions.explanation: Ongoing action over a period: have been talking.

questions.question: How long ___ she been sleeping?
questions.explanation: "How long has she been sleeping?" — present perfect continuous.

questions.question: I ___ to finish this report since 9am. (try / have been trying / tried)
questions.explanation: "Since" 9am — ongoing effort from that point until now.

questions.question: My hands are dirty because I ___ the car.
questions.explanation: Present result explained by recent continuous action: have been fixing.

title: Reported Speech — She said that...

questions.question: He said, "I am tired." → He said that he ___ tired.
questions.explanation: Reported speech: present simple → past simple. "am" becomes "was".

questions.question: She said, "I will call you." → She said she ___ call me. (will / would / could)
questions.explanation: Reported speech: "will" becomes "would".

questions.question: He said, "I have finished." → He said he ___ finished.
questions.explanation: Reported speech: present perfect → past perfect. "has" becomes "had".

questions.question: She said, "I can swim." → She said she ___ swim.
questions.explanation: Reported speech: "can" becomes "could".

questions.question: He said, "I went to Paris." → He said he ___ to Paris. (went / had gone / goes)
questions.explanation: Reported speech: past simple → past perfect. "went" becomes "had gone".

questions.question: She said, "I am working." → She said she ___ working.
questions.explanation: Reported speech: present continuous → past continuous. "am" becomes "was".

questions.question: He asked, "Do you like coffee?" → He asked me if I ___ coffee. (like / liked / likes)
questions.explanation: Reported questions: present simple → past simple.

questions.question: She said, "I don't know." → She said she ___ know.
questions.explanation: Reported speech: "don't" → "didn't".

questions.question: He said, "I must leave." → He said he ___ leave.
questions.explanation: Reported speech: "must" becomes "had to".

questions.question: She asked, "Where do you live?" → She asked me where I ___. (live / lived / living)
questions.explanation: Reported question: present simple → past simple, word order changes.

questions.question: He said, "I will help you." → He said he ___ help me.
questions.explanation: "Will" → "would" in reported speech.

questions.question: She said, "I can't come." → She said she ___ come. (can't / couldn't / wouldn't)
questions.explanation: "Can't" → "couldn't" in reported speech.

questions.question: He told me, "Don't worry." → He told me ___ worry.
questions.explanation: Reported imperative: told me + not to + base verb.

questions.question: She asked, "Are you coming?" → She asked if I ___ coming.
questions.explanation: Reported question: present continuous → past continuous.

questions.question: He said, "I have never been to Japan." → He said he ___ never been to Japan. (has / had / have)
questions.explanation: "Has never been" → "had never been" in reported speech.

questions.question: She said, "I need help." → She said she ___ help.
questions.explanation: Present simple → past simple: "needs" → "needed".

questions.question: He asked me, "What time is it?" → He asked me what time ___ . (it is / it was / is it)
questions.explanation: Reported question: present → past, normal word order (not question order).

questions.question: She told me, "Come early." → She told me ___ early.
questions.explanation: Reported imperative: told me + to + base verb.

questions.question: He said, "I was sleeping." → He said he ___ sleeping. (was / had been / is)
questions.explanation: Past continuous → past perfect continuous in reported speech.

questions.question: She asked, "Have you eaten?" → She asked if I ___ eaten.
questions.explanation: Present perfect → past perfect: "have eaten" → "had eaten".

title: Past Perfect Simple — had done, had left

questions.question: When I arrived at the cinema, the movie ___. I missed the beginning.
questions.explanation: Past perfect = had + past participle. The movie started BEFORE I arrived.

questions.question: She was hungry because she ___ breakfast. (didn't have / hadn't had / hasn't had)
questions.explanation: Past perfect negative: hadn't + past participle. She didn't eat before she became hungry.

questions.question: By the time the ambulance arrived, the patient ___.
questions.explanation: "By the time" signals that one past event happened before another → past perfect.

questions.question: He couldn't pay the electricity bill because he ___ his wallet at home.
questions.explanation: Leaving the wallet happened BEFORE the bill situation → past perfect: had left.

questions.question: I recognised her because we ___ before. (met / have met / had met)
questions.explanation: Meeting happened before the moment of recognising → past perfect: had met.

questions.question: The landlord was angry because the tenants ___ the rent for three months.
questions.explanation: Not paying happened over a period before the landlord's anger → past perfect.

questions.question: Before she got the promotion, she ___ for the company for five years. (worked / has worked / had worked)
questions.explanation: Working happened before the promotion → past perfect: had worked.

questions.question: When the doctor examined him, he realised the patient ___ the wrong medication.
questions.explanation: Taking the wrong medication happened before the examination → past perfect.

questions.question: I ___ already ___ the dishes when my roommate offered to help.
questions.explanation: Past perfect: had + past participle. Washing was completed before the offer.

questions.question: She felt relieved after she ___ her driving test. (passed / has passed / had passed)
questions.explanation: Passing the test happened before the feeling of relief → past perfect.

questions.question: The supermarket was closed. It ___ at 9pm.
questions.explanation: Shutting happened before the moment of finding it closed → past perfect.

questions.question: He applied for the apartment but someone ___ already ___ it. (has / rented / had / rented)
questions.explanation: Someone rented it before he applied → past perfect: had already rented.

questions.question: By the time she called the plumber, the pipe ___ and flooded the bathroom.
questions.explanation: The pipe bursting happened before calling the plumber → past perfect.

questions.question: I ___ never ___ sushi before I moved to the US.
questions.explanation: Past perfect for an experience (or lack of it) before a past time reference.

questions.question: The nurse checked whether the patient ___ the consent form. (signed / has signed / had signed)
questions.explanation: Signing happened before the nurse's check → past perfect.

questions.question: After she ___ the interview, she felt very confident.
questions.explanation: "After she had finished" — past perfect after "after" to show sequence clearly.

questions.question: The mechanic said someone ___ already tried to fix the brakes. (has / had)
questions.explanation: Past perfect reported: had + past participle. The attempt happened before the mechanic's discovery.

questions.question: I was late to the job interview because I ___ the wrong address.
questions.explanation: Writing the wrong address happened before being late → past perfect.

questions.question: She ___ the prescription before the pharmacy closed. (collected / had collected / has collected)
questions.explanation: Collecting the prescription happened before the pharmacy closed → past perfect.

questions.question: When I checked my bank account, I realised someone ___ money without my permission.
questions.explanation: Withdrawing happened before checking the account → past perfect: had withdrawn.

title: Past Perfect Continuous — had been doing

questions.question: She was exhausted because she ___ all night.
questions.explanation: Past perfect continuous = had been + verb-ing. Action before and up to a past point.

questions.question: His hands were dirty because he ___ the car engine. (repaired / was repairing / had been repairing)
questions.explanation: Evidence of a past continuous action that ended just before the observation.

questions.question: When the doctor finally saw her, she ___ in the waiting room for two hours.
questions.explanation: Duration of waiting BEFORE the doctor saw her → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: They ___ for a new apartment for months before they finally found one.
questions.explanation: Continuous search over a period before a past result → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: He felt sick because he ___ in the sun for hours without water. (stood / was standing / had been standing)
questions.explanation: The standing caused the sickness — it happened over a period before he felt ill.

questions.question: The construction workers ___ on the road for weeks before the project was cancelled.
questions.explanation: Continuous work over a period before the cancellation → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: I could tell she ___ because her eyes were red. (cried / was crying / had been crying)
questions.explanation: Evidence (red eyes) of a past continuous action that had just stopped.

questions.question: How long ___ you ___ for a job before you got this one?
questions.explanation: Duration of job searching before getting the job → past perfect continuous question.

questions.question: She ___ at the same pharmacy for ten years when it suddenly closed down.
questions.explanation: Ten years of continuous work before the closure → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: The baby ___ for a long time before the parents woke up. (cried / was crying / had been crying)
questions.explanation: Duration of crying before the parents woke up → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: I was out of breath because I ___ to catch the bus.
questions.explanation: Running caused breathlessness — it happened before the observation → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: They ___ about the lease agreement for weeks before both sides agreed. (negotiated / were negotiating / had been negotiating)
questions.explanation: Ongoing negotiation over weeks before reaching agreement → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: The patient told the nurse she ___ chest pain for three days before coming to the emergency room.
questions.explanation: Duration of symptoms before arriving at hospital → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: When the power cut happened, we ___ the report for six hours.
questions.explanation: Continuous writing for six hours before the power cut → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: The pipes burst because the temperature ___ below zero for days. (was / stayed / had been staying)
questions.explanation: Continuous cold temperature over days before the pipes burst → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: She got the scholarship because she ___ extremely hard all year.
questions.explanation: Studying led to the scholarship — continuous effort before the result.

questions.question: The neighbours complained because the dog ___ all night. (barked / was barking / had been barking)
questions.explanation: Continuous barking through the night before the complaint → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: He smelled of paint because he ___ the apartment all morning.
questions.explanation: Evidence of a past continuous action: painting caused the smell.

questions.question: By the time the fire brigade arrived, the fire ___ for twenty minutes. (burned / was burning / had been burning)
questions.explanation: Duration of fire burning before the fire brigade arrived → past perfect continuous.

questions.question: I was nervous at my job interview because I ___ for the position for months without success.
questions.explanation: Months of unsuccessful applications before the interview → past perfect continuous.

title: Future Continuous — will be doing

questions.question: At this time tomorrow, I ___ on a plane to Bangkok.
questions.explanation: Future continuous = will be + verb-ing. For an action in progress at a specific future time.

questions.question: Don't call me at 9am — I ___ a job interview then. (have / will have / will be having)
questions.explanation: Future continuous for an action in progress at a future time: will be having.

questions.question: This time next week, she ___ her new apartment.
questions.explanation: "This time next week" + action in progress → future continuous.

questions.question: When you arrive at the clinic, the doctor ___ other patients, so please wait.
questions.explanation: Action in progress at the moment of your arrival → future continuous.

questions.question: Don't worry about cooking tonight — I ___ home late from work. (come / will come / will be coming)
questions.explanation: Future continuous for a planned ongoing situation: will be coming home late.

questions.question: ___ you ___ past the pharmacy? Could you pick up my prescription?
questions.explanation: "Will you be passing?" — future continuous for a polite request about someone's plans.

questions.question: At 8pm tonight, the whole family ___ dinner together.
questions.explanation: Specific future time + action in progress → future continuous: will be having.

questions.question: By the time you read this message, I ___ already ___. (will / left / will be / leaving)
questions.explanation: Future continuous for an action expected to be in progress at a future reference point.

questions.question: Don't disturb her between 2 and 4pm — she ___ for her driving test.
questions.explanation: Action ongoing during a specific future time period → future continuous.

questions.question: This time next year, she ___ at a hospital in London.
questions.explanation: "This time next year" + action in progress → future continuous.

questions.question: The landlord ___ the apartment at noon, so please tidy up by then. (inspects / will inspect / will be inspecting)
questions.explanation: Action in progress at a specific future time: will be inspecting.

questions.question: ___ you ___ this way tomorrow? I need a lift to the hospital.
questions.explanation: "Will you be going this way?" — polite future continuous question about plans.

questions.question: In two hours, the surgery ___ and the patient will be in recovery.
questions.explanation: Action in progress at a specific future time point → future continuous.

questions.question: I can't meet you at 6pm — I ___ my evening class then. (attend / will attend / will be attending)
questions.explanation: Future continuous for an action in progress at a specific time.

questions.question: When the guests arrive, we ___ the last preparations for the party.
questions.explanation: Action in progress at the moment guests arrive → future continuous.

questions.question: She ___ her internship this time next month.
questions.explanation: "This time next month" + action in progress → future continuous.

questions.question: The nurse ___ rounds at midnight, so try to sleep before then. (does / will do / will be doing)
questions.explanation: Future continuous for an action planned to be in progress at a specific time.

questions.question: I ___ the immigration office all morning, so I won't be reachable.
questions.explanation: Action ongoing throughout a future morning period → future continuous.

questions.question: At this time next Saturday, we ___ our English proficiency test. (take / will take / will be taking)
questions.explanation: "At this time next Saturday" + action in progress → future continuous.

questions.question: I won't be able to answer my phone at 3pm — I ___ with my manager then.
questions.explanation: Action in progress at a specific future time: will be meeting.

title: Future Perfect — will have done

questions.question: By 6pm, I ___ all my assignments.
questions.explanation: Future perfect = will have + past participle. Action completed before a future time.

questions.question: By the time you arrive, the surgeon ___ the operation. (finishes / will finish / will have finished)
questions.explanation: "By the time you arrive" = before that future moment → future perfect.

questions.question: She ___ her nursing degree by next summer.
questions.explanation: "By next summer" = before that future point → future perfect: will have completed.

questions.question: By the end of this month, he ___ in the US for exactly one year.
questions.explanation: "By the end of this month" marks the completion point → future perfect.

questions.question: By the time she retires, she ___ for the hospital for 30 years. (works / will work / will have worked)
questions.explanation: Future perfect for a duration completed by a future point: will have worked.

questions.question: ___ you ___ the immigration forms by Friday?
questions.explanation: Future perfect question: will + subject + have + past participle.

questions.question: Don't worry — by the time your appointment starts, the waiting area ___ already ___.
questions.explanation: Completion before a future time → future perfect: will have cleared.

questions.question: By next year, she ___ enough money to pay off her student loan. (saves / will save / will have saved)
questions.explanation: "By next year" = before that future point → future perfect: will have saved.

questions.question: By the time the pharmacy opens, I ___ outside for over an hour.
questions.explanation: Future perfect continuous for duration up to a future point: will have been waiting.

questions.question: She ___ all the patients by noon if she starts early.
questions.explanation: Completion of seeing patients by a future time → future perfect.

questions.question: By the time the lease ends, we ___ in this apartment for three years. (live / will live / will have lived)
questions.explanation: Duration completed by a future point: will have lived.

questions.question: I ___ this English course by the end of the year.
questions.explanation: "By the end of the year" → future perfect: will have finished.

questions.question: By 2030, scientists ___ a cure for many more diseases. (discover / will discover / will have discovered)
questions.explanation: "By 2030" marks the completion point → future perfect.

questions.question: How many patients ___ the doctor ___ by the end of the day?
questions.explanation: Future perfect question for number of completions by end of day.

questions.question: By next month, she ___ her driving licence and can drive to work.
questions.explanation: "By next month" = before that future point → future perfect: will have got.

questions.question: He ___ the report before the board meeting starts. (submits / will submit / will have submitted)
questions.explanation: Completion before a future event → future perfect: will have submitted.

questions.question: By the time you finish reading this contract, I ___ already ___ the lease agreement.
questions.explanation: Future perfect for completion before a reference future moment.

questions.question: By graduation, she ___ over 500 hours of clinical practice. (completes / will complete / will have completed)
questions.explanation: "By graduation" = before that future point → future perfect.

questions.question: Don't call before 8pm — I ___ home by then.
questions.explanation: Negative future perfect: won't have got = arrival not completed by that time.

questions.question: By the time she turns 30, she ___ three different countries.
questions.explanation: Future perfect for experience/completion by a future age.

title: Zero Conditional — Facts & General Truths

questions.question: If you heat water to 100°C, it ___.
questions.explanation: Zero conditional: if + present simple, present simple. For facts and things always true.

questions.question: If you don't eat for a long time, you ___ hungry. (feel / will feel / would feel)
questions.explanation: Zero conditional: both clauses use present simple for universal truths.

questions.question: Plants die if they ___ enough water.
questions.explanation: Zero conditional: present simple in both clauses. A natural fact.

questions.question: If you mix red and blue, you ___ purple.
questions.explanation: Scientific/artistic fact → zero conditional: present simple in both parts.

questions.question: If the body temperature goes above 38°C, the patient ___ a fever. (has / will have / would have)
questions.explanation: Medical fact → zero conditional: present simple in both clauses.

questions.question: Ice melts if you ___ it in warm water.
questions.explanation: Physical fact → zero conditional: present simple in the if-clause.

questions.question: If you press this button, the door ___. (opens / will open / would open)
questions.explanation: General operating fact → zero conditional: present simple result.

questions.question: You feel dizzy if you ___ up too quickly.
questions.explanation: General bodily fact → zero conditional: present simple.

questions.question: If you don't pay your rent, your landlord ___ the right to evict you.
questions.explanation: Legal/general fact → zero conditional: both clauses present simple.

questions.question: If metals get very cold, they ___. (contract / will contract / contracted)
questions.explanation: Scientific fact → zero conditional: present simple in both parts.

questions.question: Germs spread quickly if people ___ their hands.
questions.explanation: Health fact → zero conditional: present simple in both clauses.

questions.question: If you ___ dairy products, you might be lactose intolerant. (can't digest / won't digest / couldn't digest)
questions.explanation: Medical general truth → zero conditional: present simple.

questions.question: Dogs ___ uncomfortable if the weather is too hot.
questions.explanation: General fact about animals → zero conditional: present simple result.

questions.question: If you mix bleach and ammonia, it ___ dangerous fumes.
questions.explanation: Chemical fact → zero conditional: present simple.

questions.question: Bread goes mouldy quickly if you ___ it in a warm place. (store / will store / stored)
questions.explanation: Food science fact → zero conditional: present simple in both parts.

questions.question: If a patient ___ their medication, symptoms can return.
questions.explanation: Medical general truth → zero conditional: present simple.

questions.question: Children learn languages faster if they ___ young. (start / will start / started)
questions.explanation: General educational truth → zero conditional: present simple.

questions.question: Blood pressure rises if you ___ too much salt.
questions.explanation: Medical fact → zero conditional: present simple in both clauses.

questions.question: If you leave food out of the fridge in summer, it ___ bad quickly. (goes / will go / went)
questions.explanation: General fact about food → zero conditional: present simple.

questions.question: If the traffic light is red, you ___.
questions.explanation: Traffic rule — always true → zero conditional: present simple.

title: Used to & Would — Past Habits & States

questions.question: I ___ walk to school every day when I was a child.
questions.explanation: "Used to" = a past habit that no longer happens. I don't walk to school anymore.

questions.question: She ___ live in a small village before she moved to the city. (used to / would / was used to)
questions.explanation: "Used to" for past states (living somewhere). "Would" cannot be used for states.

questions.question: Every summer, we ___ visit our grandparents in the countryside.
questions.explanation: For repeated past actions, both "used to" and "would" are correct.

questions.question: He ___ be very shy, but now he is very confident.
questions.explanation: "Used to" for past states. "Would" cannot describe past states like being shy.

questions.question: When I first came to the US, I ___ get lost on the subway all the time. (used to / would / both are correct)
questions.explanation: Repeated past action → both "used to get lost" and "would get lost" are correct.

questions.question: My father ___ smoke two packets a day. He quit ten years ago.
questions.explanation: "Used to smoke" — past habit that has stopped. Also "would smoke" is possible here.

questions.question: Did you ___ have a pet when you were growing up? (use to / used to / would)
questions.explanation: In questions with "did", use the base form: "Did you use to...?" (not "used to").

questions.question: I ___ not used to eating spicy food, but I love it now.
questions.explanation: Negative: "didn't use to" = I used to eat non-spicy food, but things have changed.

questions.question: On weekends, my mother ___ make a big breakfast for the whole family.
questions.explanation: Regular past action → both "used to make" and "would make" are correct.

questions.question: She ___ own a car, but she sold it when she moved to the city. (used to / would / was used to)
questions.explanation: "Used to own" — past state (owning). "Would" cannot be used for states.

questions.question: I ___ spend hours at the library after school.
questions.explanation: Repeated past action → both "used to spend" and "would spend" are correct.

questions.question: He ___ be a professional footballer before his injury.
questions.explanation: "Used to be" for past states/occupations. "Would" cannot describe what someone used to be.

questions.question: My grandparents ___ write letters because there were no mobile phones. (used to / would / both are correct)
questions.explanation: Repeated past habit → both forms are possible.

questions.question: There ___ be a market on this street every Saturday.
questions.explanation: Regular past event → both "used to be" and "would be" are possible.

questions.question: I ___ eat fast food every day, but now I cook at home. (used to / would / both)
questions.explanation: Repeated past habit → both "used to eat" and "would eat" are correct.

questions.question: She ___ have long hair when she was younger.
questions.explanation: "Used to have" — past state (having long hair). States cannot use "would".

questions.question: Every evening, my father ___ read us a bedtime story. (used to / would / both)
questions.explanation: Regular past repeated action → both forms are correct.

questions.question: ___ you use to share a bedroom with your siblings?
questions.explanation: Past habit question: Did + subject + use to (base form, no -d).

questions.question: He ___ know all his neighbours by name, but the area has changed. (used to / would / was used to)
questions.explanation: "Used to know" — past state (knowing). States cannot use "would".

questions.question: My parents ___ take us to the park every Sunday without fail.
questions.explanation: Regular past family habit → both "used to take" and "would take" are correct.

title: So / Such... that — Expressing Degree

questions.question: The waiting room was ___ crowded that people were standing in the corridor.
questions.explanation: "So + adjective + that" expresses degree or result: so crowded that...

questions.question: It was ___ a long queue that I left and came back later.
questions.explanation: "Such + a/an + adjective + noun + that" for result: such a long queue that...

questions.question: The rent was ___ expensive that I had to find a roommate. (so / such / too)
